statute: Mississippi Code § 31-3-1 et seq. (Mississippi State Board of Contractors)
license_label: Mississippi State Board of Contractors License Number
license_notes: Commercial projects over $50,000 require license. Residential new construction over $50,000 requires license. Residential remodeling/roofing over $10,000 requires license. Electrical/plumbing/HVAC under $10,000 on residential = no state license needed (local may apply).

mandatory_clauses.label: Mississippi contractor license disclosure
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Unlicensed contracting is illegal. No lien rights at all for unlicensed contractors.
notes: Mississippi requires license for commercial projects over $50,000, residential new construction over $50,000, residential remodeling over $10,000, and roofing over $10,000. Electrical/plumbing/HVAC under $10,000 on residential exempt from state license. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors. Reciprocity with GA, TN, LA, NC.
